verbatim_quote: The gene encoding the secreted 53-kDa metalloprotease (protease B) and the 5' end of the gene encoding the secreted 55-kDa metalloprotease (protease C) of the Gram-negative bacterium Erwinia chrysanthemi have been sequenced. The predicted sequences of the two proteases do not have typical signal sequences at their NH2 termini. Both proteases are synthesized as inactive higher molecular weight precursors (zymogens proB and proC) which are secreted into the external medium where divalent cation-mediated activation occurs.
notes: Delepelaire & Wandersman 1989 JBC: primary characterization of E. chrysanthemi protease B secreted signal-peptide-independently (T1SS/ABC route); current lipase DOI did not name prtB.

verbatim_quote: One of the secreted proteins is PrtA, which is shown here to be a repeats-in-toxin (RTX) alkaline zinc metalloprotease... The prtA gene resides in a locus encoding both the protease ABC transporter (prtBCD) and an intervening ORF encoding a protease inhibitor (inh).
notes: Bowen et al. 2003: Photorhabdus PrtA secreted via its ABC transporter (prtBCD = T1SS). Row gene 'prtA1' vs paper 'prtA' (same protease); current lipase DOI did not cover Photorhabdus.

organism: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (strain ATCC 15692 / DSM 22644 / CIP 104116 / JCM 14847 / LMG 12228 / 1C / PRS 101 / PAO1)
verbatim_quote: The extracellular alkaline protease produced by Pseudomonas aeruginosa is secreted by a specific pathway, independent of the pathway used by most of the other extracellular proteins of this organism. Secretion of this protease is dependent on the presence of several genes located adjacent to the apr gene... The secretion functions involved in alpha-haemolysin secretion in E. coli (hlyB, hlyD, tolC) can also be used to complement alkaline protease secretion by E. coli.
notes: Guzzo et al. 1991: P. aeruginosa alkaline protease (aprA) secreted by HlyB-family ABC/T1SS apparatus; current lipase DOI did not name aprA.

verbatim_quote: Inactivation of celA, encoding a member of the family-5 of glycosyl hydrolases, abolished the endoglucanase activity in L. pneumophila culture supernatants. The cloned celA gene conferred activity upon recombinant Escherichia coli. Thus, CelA is the major secreted endoglucanase of L. pneumophila.
notes: Pearce & Cianciotto 2009 (FEMS Microbiol Lett), PMID 19817866. lspF mutant deficient = T2SS substrate. Current DOI was a 16S rRNA ecology paper (wrong topic).

verbatim_quote: Infection of eukaryotic cells with Y. enterocolitica strains expressing a Ysp-CyaA chimeric protein resulted in Ysa T3S system-dependent increases in cytosolic levels of cAMP for six Ysps (YspK, YspI, YspE, YspF, YspP and YspL)
notes: Matsumoto & Young 2006 Mol Microbiol (PMID 16390460): YspE translocated by the Ysa T3SS of Y. enterocolitica 1B (CyaA-reporter). Current DOI 10.1128/JB.01816-08 fails CrossRef/handle resolution; resolved to the primary suite-characterization paper that names YspE verbatim. Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

verbatim_quote: Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB. Orf19 is targeted to host mitochondria, where it appears to interfere with the ability to maintain membrane potential.
notes: Kenny & Jepson 2000 (Cell Microbiol), PMID 11207610 - primary demonstration that this LEE-encoded T3SS effector (orf19, upstream of tir) is translocated and mitochondria-targeted. 'Orf19' renamed Map by Kenny et al. 2002 (PMID 12046591); Creasey 2003 (10.1046/j.1365-2958.2003.03290.x) states 'Map ... is translocated into eukaryotic cells by a type III secretion system.' Map LEE-conserved in O157:H7 Sakai. Current DOI was a Toxoplasma gondii paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: We discovered that orf 18 of the enterocyte effacement locus encodes a new effector, which we termed EspH. We show that EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
notes: Tu et al. 2003 (Mol Microbiol), PMID 12535063 - primary discovery of EspH as LEE(orf18)-encoded T3SS effector in EHEC+EPEC. Current DOI was a cytolethal-distending toxin paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: A SepZ-CyaA fusion was secreted and translocated into HeLa cells in a TTSS-dependent manner... To indicate its newly identified status as a translocated effector protein, we propose to rename SepZ as EspZ.
notes: Kanack et al. 2005 (Infect Immun), PMID 15972527, 'SepZ/EspZ is secreted and translocated ... by the EPEC type III secretion system.' Current DOI was the Vibrio cholerae T6SS-discovery PNAS paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB. Orf19 is targeted to host mitochondria, where it appears to interfere with the ability to maintain membrane potential.
notes: Kenny & Jepson 2000 (Cell Microbiol), PMID 11207610 - EPEC O127:H6 E2348/69 (exact organism). orf19 renamed Map; Creasey 2003 corroborates T3SS translocation. Current DOI was a Toxoplasma gondii paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: We discovered that orf 18 of the enterocyte effacement locus encodes a new effector, which we termed EspH. We show that EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
notes: Tu et al. 2003 (Mol Microbiol), PMID 12535063 - EPEC O127:H6 E2348/69. Current DOI was a cytolethal-distending toxin paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: Shigella deliver a subset of effectors into the host cell via the type III secretion system... Here we show that one of the Shigella effectors, called VirA, can interact with tubulin to promote microtubule (MT) destabilization
notes: Yoshida et al. 2002 EMBO J (PMID 12065406): VirA is a Shigella T3SS-translocated effector. Current DOI 10.1126/science.1116848 was a mammalian tooth-patterning paper (wrong topic). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

verbatim_quote: Multinucleated giant cell formation and apoptosis in infected host cells is mediated by Burkholderia pseudomallei type III secretion protein BipB.
notes: Suparak et al. 2005 J Bacteriol (PMID 16159789): BipB is a Bsa T3SS protein (type-concordant; confirms the prior manual 'sound biology' verdict but the original cited DOI 10.1099/mic.0.26396-0 was a colicin/microcin paper = wrong). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked on B. pseudomallei content).

verbatim_quote: CopN, but not NrdB, was secreted by Y. enterocolitica in a Ca2+- and pYV-dependent fashion. These data indicate that components of the putative type III apparatus of C. trachomatis are expressed and that at least one of these products is secreted by chlamydiae to the inclusion membrane.
notes: Fields & Hackstadt 2000 Mol Microbiol (PMID 11123678), 'Evidence for the secretion of Chlamydia trachomatis CopN by a type III secretion mechanism.' Current DOI 10.1074/jbc.M610343200 fails CrossRef/handle resolution. Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

ChlaDub1/Cdu1 (Chlamydia trachomatis deubiquitinase). The canonical primary paper, Misaghi et al. 2006 Mol Microbiol (PMID 16824101, 10.1111/j.1365-2958.2006.05199.x), establishes ONLY deubiquitinating/deneddylating activity; it does NOT demonstrate T3SS secretion/translocation. No primary paper found establishing ChlaDub1 as a T3SS-secreted effector. T3SS classification unsupported (matches the prior manual audit). Current DOI 10.1126/science.aag1029 fails resolution (likely typo). Handled in main session (batch-C agent AUP-blocked).

verbatim_quote: Here, we report a detailed investigation of a P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS-dependent phospholipase effector, TplE, which acts as a trans-kingdom toxin. Delivery of TplE to the periplasmic space of rival bacteria leads to growth inhibition.
notes: Current DOI 10.1038/nature11600 is WRONG (Koga et al., protein-structure-design paper). PA1510 = Tle4 = TplE (Tli4 immunity = PA1509). Tle4 name from Russell 2013 (10.1038/nature12074) which lists PA1510 informatically but validated only Tle1/2/5. Primary experimental T6SS-secretion of PA1510 = Jiang et al. 2016 Cell Reports (PMID 27477276). DUPLICATE of idx 24 (same PA1510).

verbatim_quote: Consistent with this hypothesis, we identified a B. pseudomallei type III secreted protein, BopA, which plays a role in mediating bacterial evasion of autophagy.
notes: BopA is a Bsa TYPE III secretion effector, NOT T6SS. Misassigned. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2024.04.012 (Szczesna 'Bacterial esterases reverse LPS ubiquitylation') is about TssM, only mentions BopA in a GFP panel. Primary: Cullinane et al. 2008 Autophagy (10.4161/auto.6246). Name-collision warning correct.

verbatim_quote: Furthermore, we provide evidence that one of the two T6SS nanotube subunits, Hcp1, is required for killing Klebsiella oxytoca in vitro and that this activity is mediated by the specific interaction of Hcp1 with the antibacterial amidase Tae4.
notes: Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2012.06.002 is a Drosophila PGRP-LE immunity paper (wrong). Correct: Sana et al. PNAS 2016 (PMID 27503894), SPI-6 T6SS amidase Tae4 of S. Typhimurium 14028.

verbatim_quote: We characterized a Salmonella T6SS antibacterial effector called Tlde1 that is toxic in target-cell periplasm and is neutralized by its cognate immunity protein (Tldi1).
notes: Current DOI correct (Sibinelli-Sousa et al. Cell Reports 2020, PMID 32579939). Tlde1 = Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS L,D-transpeptidase. Row gene 'Tlde1A' is paralog locus STM14_RS02030.

verbatim_quote: We further identify Tle1, a toxin effector encoded by this cluster and showed that Tle1 possesses phospholipase A1 and A2 activities required for the interbacterial competition.
notes: Current DOI 10.3389/fcimb.2018.00074 is an HSV paper (wrong). Correct: Flaugnatti et al. Mol Microbiol 2016 (PMID 26714038), Tle1 phospholipase A1 effector of EAEC 042 Sci-1 T6SS (EC042_RS24190).

P. entomophila AprA is experimentally shown secreted (Liehl 2006, doi 10.1371/journal.ppat.0020056) but no primary paper experimentally demonstrates T1SS/ABC export of the P. entomophila ortholog specifically; T1SS assignment is by homology to P. aeruginosa AprA.

organism: Bordetella bronchiseptica (strain ATCC BAA-588 / NCTC 13252 / RB50) (Alcaligenes bronchisepticus)
verbatim_quote: As for E. coli alpha-haemolysin, secretion of B.pertussis adenylate cyclase and haemolysin requires the expression of additional genes... requiring, in addition to the cyaB and cyaD gene products, the presence of a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene.
notes: Glaser et al. 1988 EMBO J: CyaA secreted by cyaBDE T1SS. Primary experimental work is in B. pertussis; this row is the B. bronchiseptica ortholog (cyaABDE operon conserved). Current E-cadherin DOI was wrong-topic.

organism: Bordetella pertussis (strain Tohama I / ATCC BAA-589 / NCTC 13251)
verbatim_quote: As for E. coli alpha-haemolysin, secretion of B.pertussis adenylate cyclase and haemolysin requires the expression of additional genes... requiring, in addition to the cyaB and cyaD gene products, the presence of a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene.
notes: Glaser et al. 1988 EMBO J: B. pertussis CyaA (cyclolysin) secreted by cyaBDE T1SS (HlyB/HlyD homologues). Exact organism match. Current E-cadherin DOI was wrong-topic.

verbatim_quote: We previously showed that Legionella pneumophila secretes, via its type II secretion system, phospholipase A activities that are distinguished by their specificity for certain phospholipids. In this study, we identified and characterized plaA, a gene encoding a phospholipase A that cleaves fatty acids from lysophospholipids... implying that PlaA is the major secreted lysophospholipase A of L. pneumophila.
notes: Flieger et al. 2002 (Infect Immun), PMID 12379686. Primary characterization of plaA as the major T2SS-secreted lysophospholipase A. Current DOI was a 16S rRNA ecology paper (wrong topic).

verbatim_quote: Here, we report a detailed investigation of a P. aeruginosa H2-T6SS-dependent phospholipase effector, TplE, which acts as a trans-kingdom toxin. Delivery of TplE to the periplasmic space of rival bacteria leads to growth inhibition.
notes: Current DOI 10.1016/j.celrep.2016.07.020 is WRONG (Liu 'MAPK ... Pulmonary Alveolar Regeneration'). Correct differs by last digits: 2016.07.012 (Jiang et al. 2016 Cell Reports, PMID 27477276), TplE=PA1510=Tle4 H2-T6SS effector. DUPLICATE of idx 15.

verbatim_quote: We report the characterization of BopE, a type III secreted protein that is encoded adjacent to the Burkholderia pseudomallei bsa locus and is homologous to Salmonella enterica SopE/SopE2.
notes: BopE is a Bsa TYPE III secretion effector (Cdc42/Rac1 GEF), NOT T6SS. Misassigned. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2024.04.012 is about TssM, mentions BopE only in a GFP panel. Primary: Stevens et al. 2003 J Bacteriol (10.1128/JB.185.16.4992-4996.2003). Name-collision warning correct.

Locus resolves to BTH_II0090 (DUF3274 protein) but is NOT named in the current_doi (Russell et al. 2012, PMC3358704); not among that paper's named T6SS-1 substrates. No experimental T6SS-effector support. Unsupported/unidentifiable.

verbatim_quote: Structure-based algorithms predict extensive structural similarity between two of the proteins, BTH_II0310 and BTH_I2691, and bacteriocins LlpA and colicin Ia, respectively
notes: current_doi verified (Russell et al. 2012, Cell Host Microbe, PMC3358704). BTH_I2691 named as one of the 11 proteins requiring T6SS-1 for export. Not individually biochemically characterized but named verbatim. prior_audit 'unidentifiable_row' incorrect: locus identifiable + supported.

verbatim_quote: These data show that Tle1 is an antibacterial effector delivered between cells by T6S, and that I2699, henceforth referred to as Tli1 (type VI secretion lipase immunity 1), protects against Tle1.
notes: Tle1 = BTH_I2698 (B. thailandensis E264), T6SS-1 phospholipase effector. Current DOI 10.1016/j.chom.2012.04.007 (Russell 2012) is a real T6SS paper but does NOT name Tle1; the Tle nomenclature + experimental delivery come from Russell et al. 2013 Nature (10.1038/nature12074, PMID 23552891).
Locus resolves to BTH_I3226 (DUF3274 protein), NOT named in current_doi (Russell et al. 2012, PMC3358704); not among named T6SS-1 substrates. No primary paper found. Unsupported/unidentifiable.

verbatim_quote: Here, we report that Burkholderia thailandensis T6SS4 is involved in zinc acquisition via secretion of a zinc-scavenging protein, TseZ, that interacts with the outer membrane heme transporter HmuR.
notes: Current DOI correct (Si et al. Cell Reports 2017, PMID 28746878). TseZ = T6SS4-secreted zinc-scavenging protein, B. thailandensis (BTH_RS09645).
